item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the upvote! If the article implies that the cult/bird were responsible then that's a mistake on my part, it's more of a "happy" accident that the Foundation found out about the group in the first place due to Chernobyl, I probably could've made that clearer by mentioning the reports from witnesses on the ground, maybe an addendum that says no signs of the cult/bird were found in the power plant?

Thank you for the upvote again :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very interesting take on the Black Bird of Chernobyl! I found the majority of the article to be engaging, though it slowed down a bit during the Gamayun Documents; I don't have any feedback on how to improve it, though it's not too much of a problem since the article is nearly over at that point. The implications in the Document are worded in neat ways: "the veil shall be lifted;" "We dwell in darkness, and must regain our way in the light…" Also, I'm unsure if it is, but I hope the article isn't implying the cult and/or the Black Bird were responsible for Chernobyl.

I'm rambling. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would it be a case of more test logs, an interview, a more in-depth look at the group in question? While it's valid to dislike the article, I'd appreciate any feedback on how I could improve the execution

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't really know. Maybe its the concept well and the way the story was told. The problem wasn't on how it was all tie together but the execution. And mostly my personal taste.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the feedback! What sort of scenario or addition would've helped tie it all together in your opinion?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with Nico when talking about pacing, and the escalation was fine and somewhat intriguing to me, but all of it together doesn't really do anything for me.

This bird cult and its motives/teachings don't interest me. In the article, all we get is that there are a cult, they have a magic knife, and markings cut by that knife makes people mutate. There's no greater motive other than weird god /philosophy, and the test logs don't save the article for me.

item.title: Novote
item.content:encoded: Agreed. There's work at worldbuilding and such here, but none of it really struck me as interesting. On a similar note, I felt the experiment logs dragged on too long without properly escalating.

item.title: Re: Spelling Error
item.content:encoded: Indeed I did! Thank you very much!

item.title: Spelling Error
item.content:encoded:
D-11923 beings speaking in as of yet untranslated Russian dialect.

I assume you meant begins. Just thought I'd point this out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just had a quick glance over and there are some that slipped the net. I copypasted the article into Google docs to check for those but I mustn't have been as accurate as I should've been!

What sorta things would you suggest for adding to an article like this to make it stand out? I do agree that it does miss a spark of some kind to really kick it off.

Thanks for the feedback!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, this article still has a handful of spelling/grammatical errors that could have been found by a simple typing program. The anomaly itself is fine, and the pacing is fine too. The hook of the bird man in the discovery log foreshadows the secret logs pretty well. Overall the piece is solid, though I do find myself wondering why those symbols were added in at the bottom. They don't seem to serve much of a purpose to the article itself.

Despite all this, I can't help but feel like something is missing to really make the article pop.

item.title: SCP-4896
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to my 2nd ever SCP article!

Big thanks to: Dr Cimmerian, Fewr, nico and vazorium for help with page format and critique! Thanks to anyone else who gave me a hand that I've forgotten (Sorry!)

If there's any critique or praise you want to give feel free to drop it below!

This SCP was partially inspired by Slavic Paganism and the Gamayun creature of folkore.

Any questions feel free to ask!
